question: A beam of uniform strength has at every cross-section same
explanation: A beam of uniform strength has the same bending moment at every cross-section.
subject: Bending Moment
ai_correct: A beam of uniform strength is designed such that the bending stress at every cross-section is equal to the allowable working stress. This is achieved by varying the section modulus along the length.
ai_wrong: Students often confuse 'uniform strength' with 'uniform bending moment'. A beam of uniform strength can have a varying bending moment if the section modulus is varied accordingly.
trick: Uniform strength means constant stress, not constant moment.
